subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear articulation of the research question with independent, dependent and controlled variables specified (including units and uncertainties).
Background section situates the study in a precise post-harvest spoilage context, justifying the choice of Aloe vera against Rhizopus stolonifer.
Detailed variables table explaining control measures and justification for six replicates, demonstrating sufficiency of data collection.
weaknesses: Numerical slips in concentration calculations (e.g., 15 % â 1 g) introduce ambiguity.
Sample calculation provided only for 5 % concentration; other concentrations lack step-by-step detail.
Disk diffusion soak time stated without rationale or citation, limiting transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands meet the highest band descriptor (explained methodological considerations; specific context) while the reproducibility description has a few ambiguities (band 3â4). The combination merits a mark at the upper end of the 3â6 scale, hence 5 out of 6.

strengths: Raw and processed data are presented in well-labelled tables and graphs with means, standard deviations and RÂ² values, demonstrating clarity and precision.
Statistical tests (ANOVA, Tukey HSD) are correctly applied, reported with degrees of freedom, sum of squares and p-values, and interpreted in context.
Hypothesis table clearly distinguishes Hâ and Hâ, underpinning the rationale for the chosen statistical methods.
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation is missingâinstrument uncertainties are listed but not carried through to processed results.
Inconsistent notation in the Tukey HSD table (e.g., ââ5â instead of â5 %â) could confuse interpretation.
Minor transcription errors in the F-value formula line and an incomplete narrative on the RÂ² value reduce precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While data recording and processing are communicated with clarity and statistical methods are applied accurately (band 5), significant omissions in uncertainty propagation and notation inconsistencies place the work solidly in the 3â4 range, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question with quantitative citation of ANOVA p-value, RÂ² and inhibition diameters, fully consistent with the analysis.
Justifies findings through comparison with accepted literature on Aloe vera bioactive compounds, linking statistical results to scientific context.
Integration of graphical trends, statistical significance and literature discussion provides a coherent, wellâsubstantiated argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands meet the highest descriptor: the conclusion is fully consistent with analysis and justified through relevant scientific comparison. No significant deficiencies were noted, warranting the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (manual measurement error, extract purity, humidity control, viscosity issues).
Realistic improvements are proposed (digital calipers, fresh extract preparation, hygrometer use, less viscous extract selection).
weaknesses: The report does not explain the relative impact of each identified weakness on result validity.
Improvements lack detail on how they would quantitatively reduce uncertainty or improve reliability.
No suggestion of a positive control to benchmark Aloe vera efficacy against a standard antifungal.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student describes specific limitations and realistic improvements (band 3â4) but does not explain their relative impact or deeper rationale. Performance leans toward the lower bound of the band, justifying a score of 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly answers the research question, cites numerical results, and is fully consistent with ANOVA, Tukey and graphical analyses, thereby meeting the âjustified and fully consistentâ descriptor.

Raw data are organised in a complete table with units and uncertainties; processed data (means, SDs) are included and displayed in well-labelled graphs. The presentation is consistently legible and structured, demonstrating both clarity and precision.

Means, SDs, ANOVA and Tukey HSD are calculated correctly and applied to address the research question. Graphs incorporate an RÂ² value and trends are interpreted. Minor transcription errors do not materially affect accuracy, so processing is appropriate and largely accurate.

Specific weaknessesâmanual measurement, impurity of extract, uncontrolled humidity, viscosity issuesâare described, but their relative impacts are only briefly implied rather than thoroughly explained, placing the work in band 3-4.

The student compares findings with literature on Aloe veraâs antimicrobial action and explains bioactive compounds, linking experimental results to accepted scientific context. The comparison supports the conclusion, satisfying the highest band.

The research question is clearly stated and the background section provides detailed, organism-specific information about Rhizopus stolonifer, existing control measures and Aloe veraâs antifungal potential. This places the investigation within a well-defined, relevant post-harvest spoilage context, satisfying the descriptor for a specific and appropriate context.

Instrument uncertainties are stated and error bars use SD, but there is no propagation of ruler uncertainty and no discussion of percentage uncertainty or its impact. Thus consideration is evident yet contains significant omissions, fitting band 3-4.

Improvements (digital calipers, preparing fresh extract, using a hygrometer, selecting less viscous extract) are realistic and clearly linked to the stated limitations, but the discussion stops at description without deeper explanation of how each would enhance validity, fitting band 3-4.

The student identifies independent, dependent and key controlled variables, justifies their control in a table, and specifies six trials per treatment to obtain sufficient data. These points collectively explain how relevant and sufficient data will be collected to answer the question, meeting the âexplainedâ level.

The procedure covers spore suspension, extract preparation, inoculation, incubation and measurement in logical order, enabling reproduction. Nevertheless, concentration calculations contain numerical slips (e.g. 15 % â 1 g) and disk placement is only shown schematically, leaving a few ambiguities. Hence reproduction is possible âwith few ambiguitiesâ, aligning with band 3-4.

annotations.comment: Including error bars in the bar chart enhances data communication; consider annotating outliers or anomalous 5 % trials directly on the figure to highlight deviations.
The narrative describing the RÂ² value breaks midâsentence; complete the discussion of its statistical significance and implications for model fit.
The hypothesis table clearly distinguishes Hâ and Hâ, underpinning the statistical design and improving communication of test rationale.
The ANOVA results table reports degrees of freedom, SS and pâvalue accurately, providing robust evidence for rejecting Hâ.
The Tukey HSD table uses inconsistent notation (e.g., ââ5â for 5 %); correct concentration labels to avoid confusion in post-hoc interpretation.
The conclusion directly addresses the research question and cites quantitative findings (ANOVA p-value, RÂ², diameter range), demonstrating relevance and consistency.
Linking the RÂ² value and ANOVA results to the literature on Aloe veraâs bioactive compounds enriches scientific context comparison and validates broader relevance.
Specific methodological weaknesses (manual measurement, extract purity, humidity control, viscosity issues) are identified, but the report omits detailed discussion of their relative impact.
Including relative impact (e.g., estimate error magnitude from ruler vs. humidity variation) would clarify which limitation most critically affects results.
Proposed improvements are realistic but lack detailed explanation of how each change (e.g., digital calipers, hygrometer) would enhance validity and reduce uncertainty.
The research question articulates independent, dependent and controlled variables clearly, with specified units and uncertainties, providing a strong foundation for reproducibility.
The background section situates the study within a targeted post-harvest spoilage context, demonstrating an appropriate and specific justification for examining Aloe vera against Rhizopus stolonifer.
The variables table provides detailed controls and justifications, explaining how each factor is maintained constant to ensure relevant and sufficient data collection.
While the procedure outlines spore suspension and extract preparation, numerical slips in concentration calculations (e.g., 15 % â 1 g) undermine full reproducibility and should be corrected.
Sample calculation is provided only for 5 % concentration; include analogous stepâbyâstep calculations for other concentrations to remove ambiguity in extract preparation.
The disk diffusion exposure time (30 min) is stated without rationale; cite a source or pilot data to justify why this soak period optimizes extract absorption.
Raw data are presented comprehensively with means, standard deviations and consistent units, facilitating both clarity and precision in data recording.
Uncertainties are listed for individual instruments but propagation through to processed results is missing; include combined uncertainty calculations to assess overall reliability.
The F-value calculation in Table 4 contains transcription errors in the formula line that could mislead repeat analysis; ensure the numerator and denominator terms are formatted correctly.
Extend recommendations by specifying a positive control (e.g., fluconazole at known concentration) to benchmark Aloe veraâs efficacy against an established antifungal.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is stated without context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is outlined within a broad context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.content: The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
